title: nie-tui E2E: two instances exchange message and verify in history
description:
**What:** End-to-end integration test: start the relay, launch two nie-tui instances in headless/scripted mode (or use nie-bot-style programmatic testing), send a message from one to the other, verify it appears in the chat history DB of the receiver.

**Algorithm:**
1. Create tui/tests/e2e.rs (integration test).
2. Test setup:
   a. Start relay in background: spawn nie-relay with a temp SQLite DB and random port.
   b. Create two identity keyfiles (alice, bob) with no-passphrase.
   c. Instead of spawning nie-tui (which needs a terminal), drive the event loop directly:
      - Call transport::connect_with_retry for alice and bob.
      - Create AppState for alice and bob.
      - Run handle_relay_event in a tokio task for each (simulating the event loop without terminal).
   d. Alice sends a chat message: call send_chat() directly or construct a BroadcastParams request and send via alice.tx.
   e. Wait up to 3 seconds for bob.messages to contain a ChatLine::Chat with alice's text.
   f. Verify: assert!(bob.state.messages.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, ChatLine::Chat{text, ..} if text == "hello from alice")));
   g. Verify: history DB for bob contains the message (call history::History::open(bob_data_dir).recent(1)).
3. Teardown: terminate relay process, clean up temp dirs.

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Test must not require a real terminal — use headless event loop (no ratatui drawing).
- Test must use real relay process (not mocked) — per CLAUDE.md test oracle discipline.
- Timeout: wrap recv loop with tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...) to avoid hanging on test failure.
- Temp dirs: use tempfile::TempDir for automatic cleanup.
- Relay port: bind to port 0 (OS assigns) to avoid conflicts.
- No hardcoded expected values derived from the code under test — the expected text is an independent literal "hello from alice".

**Agent team:** Standard (R+I+V). R reads relay/tests/ for existing E2E test patterns (stress.rs, e2e.rs). I implements tui/tests/e2e.rs. V runs cargo test -p nie-tui e2e.

**Tests (the bead IS the test):**
- Two instances exchange a known message: passes.
- Timeout if message not received within 5s: test fails with clear message, not hang.
- History DB contains the message: verifies persistence.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-tui e2e_exchange -- --test-threads=1 passes; relay is started fresh (not mocked); message appears in receiver's history DB
